figures.caption: Fig. 1: Comparison between the existing methods and the proposed method. The existing methods encrypt coarse Tile-aligned regions, which may cover non-ROI, while the proposed method achieves finer ROI localization through CU-level mapping, combines PCM mode, MV restriction, and CU-level encryption to reduce distortion leakage.

Fig. 8: Visual comparison of ROI encryption results on four test sequences. Each row corresponds to one sequence: the original frame, the results encrypted by Taha [15], Zhang [5], and the proposed scheme for two representative frames. The proposed CU-level ROI encryption scheme achieves finer-grained protection and more accurate encryption localization than existing Tile-level approaches.
TABLE I MAIN SYNTAX ELEMENTS OF H.265/HEVC
Fig. 7: Visual ablation study of diffusion isolation. From left to right are the original frame, the encrypted frame without diffusion isolation, and the encrypted frame with diffusion isolation.
